markers.name: The Arcane City of Luminos
markers.notes: The Flying Arcane city of Luminos is a city that was raised from the ground by the powerful Arch-mage Celeros in the wake of the War of the Illithids. It is a safe place for mages from all across Dorenia, with its inhabitants dedicated to the study of the Arcane.

markers.name: The Free City of Nyleros
markers.notes: The New Capital of Veldenar- and the seat of the 4 noble houses (Moorcroft, Siofban, Farozan & Hawkspire) that now rule the former Kingdom of Veldenar. A merchant city with frequent vistors from Meridan across the sea.

markers.name: The City of Mestthos
markers.notes: The only major settlement in Azrakan, the City of Mestthos located along the Azzeron coast is a place of respite for many who dare to cross the great Ashar deserts. A city known for its sea-faring, fishing & relative isolation from the rest of the continent.

markers.name: The Grand City of Aethros
markers.notes: The Grand City of Aethros sits at Dorenia's North, and is the largest city on the continent. Once a small city, the wake of the Illithid War brought foreign Eryndorian investment & nobility to the city and drove it to grow and expand to an unfathomably large size. Ruled by King Serrus, and also home to "The Church of the One"

markers.name: The Ruined City of Vaelrith
markers.notes: Former Capital of the Githyanki Nalharan Empire, after it was ravaged & destroyed during the War of the Illithids its current status is unknown, as nobody has dared to set foot in the great region as a corruption has tainted this entire area.

markers.name: Thormund's Fortress
markers.notes: Deep underground, Thormund's Fortress resides like an iron stronghold- with tunnels & mineshaft's stretching far and wide across the region. Well maintained & defended- the fortress itself is nearly impenetrable, with the above-ground doors made of Mithril mined from the lowest layers of the Underdark, this underground city is a self-sustaining bastion for all dwarvenkind in Dorenia.

markers.name: The City State of Ritan
markers.notes: Capital of the New Rimmaran State, the city is the base of operations for the Rimmaran Expansion into Dorenia. Travelling by sea from the West, the Rimmaran's conquered the former kingdom of Acria and have used the region as a foothold for their conquest of Dorenia.

markers.name: Duskreach
markers.notes: Not much is publicly known about the Duskreach, except for that it is rumored to be a safe place for all of those who may be downtrodden or forced into circumstances that might require people to turn to the criminal life. Said to be created by the Infamous Smuggler Kael.

markers.name: The Elven Cities of Averlon
markers.notes: From the High-Elven city of Tel' Selae to the Drowish city of Tel' Nevae, the Elven cities of Averlon reside at the heart of the Averlonian Woods, a magical forest which protects all of its inhabitants from exterior threats. Elves have lived in relative peace and harmony since the War of the Illithids because of these woods.

markers.name: The Free City of Valrune
markers.notes: What was once initially a small coastal town of Moldath has been turned into a place for the many pirates who roam the Isles of Calyros to call home.

markers.name: The City of Sarindar
markers.notes: he City of Sarindar resides on the north-east coast of the Kingdom. The city is ancient and is filled with a storied history- and was said to have been ruled over by an Ancient Dragon at some point in history.

markers.name: The City of Islaris
markers.notes: The former capital of Veldenar, now a shell of its former glory.

markers.name: The Town of Rilos
markers.notes: A small village in the centre of Veldenar known for its mines & farming.

markers.name: The town of Kaire
markers.notes: A small town mostly known as a rest stop for merchants travelling to Nyleros.

markers.name: The Stormspire Isles
markers.notes: A series of large islets off the Veldenari coast that are quite mountainous in their nature. Some of these islets soar up to cloud-level, whilst others are barely above sea level. Various different tribal creatures live here, such as Giants, Aarakrocas, Goblins etc.

markers.name: The Town of Rova
markers.notes: A large town town located at the North-most point of Dorenia. Known for its fishing & live-stock.

markers.name: The Town of Druva
markers.notes: A small down to the South-East of Aethros. Known for its religious studies & churches now dedicated to "The One".

markers.name: Old Eronia
markers.notes: The Former Capital of Syldran that was destroyed during the War of the Illithids. Now destroyed, with the region proving very dangerous to traverse, with sightings of many different creatures here.

markers.name: The Hamlets of Chizk
markers.notes: A small series of hamlets off of the Velhearth Coast known for their fishing.

markers.name: The Great Syldran Wall
markers.notes: A Great Wall dividing Syldran from the rest of the Continent. Over 200ft tall, with watchtowers all along the wall that are constantly under the watch of the Syldran Guard.

markers.name: The Hamlet of Sal
markers.notes: A small hamlet to the east of Eronia.

markers.name: The Post
markers.notes: A small Army post along the "Golden Path" to Aethros.

markers.name: The City of Kaldrin
markers.notes: The second largest city in Syldran, The City of Kaldrin lies off of the Khaldori Coast, and is where a bulk of the Syldran Navy patrols. Currently the seas are contested between Syldranian & Rimmaran ships, with a few skirmishes occurring between the two nations.

markers.name: The Town of Velhearth
markers.notes: The Town of Velhearth is an isolated town on the Velhearth Plateu- an island to the West of the Great Kingdom of Syldran. Whilst technically under Syldrani rule, the people of Velhearth live in relative peace & harmony not under the watch of the Syldranian Armies.

markers.name: The Temple of Mythra
markers.notes: A large temple located to the North-East of Azrakan, only accessible by crossing the Great Ashar Desert.

markers.name: Paradia
markers.notes: A small post for travellers whom dare to follow the "Path of the Sun" across the Great Ashar Desert.

markers.name: Kael's Pass
markers.notes: A treacherous route discovered by the infamous smuggler Kael to get in & out of Syldran/Nalhara.

markers.name: Foldren
markers.notes: A small town along the "Golden Road" not governed by any Kingdom. Mostly a place for merchants to sell their wares to travellers along the route.

markers.name: The Eastern Syldran Settlements
markers.notes: A series of small towns & hamlets along the east of Syldran, known for their agriculture.

markers.name: Fort Morgrim
markers.notes: The Northern Fortress that lies along the Golden Road, run by the Dwarves of Thormund's Kingdom that spans through the underground of Khaldorin. Morgrim Town lies to the West along Ravenstone Cove.

markers.name: The Town of Keldrin
markers.notes: A large town mainly made up of displaces people's from around Dorenia who can't call Thormund's Fortress home. Many humans, elves, orc's & more reside here in relative peace and harmony.

markers.name: Gnolm
markers.notes: A mostly goblin settlement to the West of Druenol's chair & south of Thormund's Fortress.

markers.name: The Town of Vaskir
markers.notes: Vaksir lies just above the New Rimmaran border to Khaldorin, protected by hill's & Fort Gundrik from would-be Rimmaran invaders.

markers.name: Fort Gundrik
markers.notes: Fort Gundrik is the southern-most fortress in Khaldorin, being the first line of defense against a New Rimmaran invasion.

markers.name: Ravenstone Cove
markers.notes: A large in-land coastline that border's both Syldran & Khaldorin. Quite frigid, mostly populated by fishing boats & the Syldranian Navy.

markers.name: Druenol's Chair
markers.notes: A large series of mountains inhabited primarily by Dragon's & many Kobold tribes. Said to be the seat of Druenol himself for many years as he admired his creation.

routes.name: The Golden Road
routes.notes: A trade route that spans from the North of the Continent- The Grand City of Aethros in Syldran to the South- to the City of Sarindar.
